The solution has 139 commands. The game summary says 262 moves, so I'm still missing many. The 1fps frame rate might be missing some commands. Let me try to use the 5fps HQ frames that are already partially OCR'd (60 files done). But that might take too long.

Let me instead examine the OCR outputs more carefully to understand if I'm missing commands because the transcript scrolls commands off screen. At 1fps, each frame captures only a portion of the full transcript (maybe 4-6 commands visible at a time). So over 190 seconds, I'm seeing different snapshots of the transcript.

I need to track commands as they appear and disappear from the visible transcript. My current approach of looking for commands that are NEW in each frame should work. But I need to also include repeated commands (like 'n', 's', 'e', 'w' which are used many times).

The issue is that my script only adds a command once when it first appears. If the player types 'n' multiple times, it only appears once in my list. I need to track the sequence of commands as they are typed, including repeats.

From the OCR outputs, I can see that each frame shows the LAST command being typed at the bottom. When this changes between frames, a new command was executed. By tracking ALL changes in the typing command, I should get the full sequence including repeats.

Let me check if my tracking script is capturing repeats correctly.
